tasks.title: Product review and gap analysis
tasks.description: Thoroughly review the current product's codebase, docs, README, CLI, UX flows, features, architecture, plugin/extension points, existing roadmap and tests (assume repository root is the product workspace). Produce a concise product analysis that: 1) lists implemented features and their current maturity, 2) identifies missing features, usability pain points, and technical limitations, 3) maps existing components that could be extended/reused, and 4) gives high-level risk areas (security, scalability, license issues). Deliverables: - target/temp/swarmout/product_report.md: human-readable report (4–12 sections) summarizing findings. - target/temp/swarmout/product_report.json: machine-readable summary listing features (present/missing), priorities, and short effort estimates (T-shirt or day estimates). Ensure file outputs are written to target/temp/swarmout. Do not assume access to other agents' outputs.

tasks.title: Competitor analysis: claude-code-sourcemap
tasks.description: Independently analyze the competitor located at ../claude-code-sourcemap. Inspect README, source tree, CLI or UI, features set, integration points, sample workflows, supported languages, documentation quality, tests, licensing, and any unique UX or technical approaches. Produce: 1) a competitor report: target/temp/swarmout/claude-code-sourcemap_report.md covering features, strengths, weaknesses, standout implementations, and potential IP/license flags; 2) a summary JSON: target/temp/swarmout/claude-code-sourcemap_summary.json listing feature booleans, complexity indicators, and recommended features our product should consider adopting. In the report, explicitly list at least 6 concrete features or behaviors (with short rationale) that would be beneficial to add to our product. Keep output self-contained; do not wait for other agents.

tasks.title: Competitor analysis: copilot-cli
tasks.description: Independently analyze Copilot-CLI (assume repository name or remote source is accessible as copilot-cli). Inspect README, usage examples, core features, CLI ergonomics, integration points (editors, CI, APIs), and any automation capabilities. Produce: 1) target/temp/swarmout/copilot-cli_report.md documenting feature list, UX strengths, limitations, common workflows, and at least 6 specific features or behaviors worth adopting/adapting; 2) target/temp/swarmout/copilot-cli_summary.json with structured feature flags, estimated implementation complexity, and priority recommendations. Highlight differences between interactive and batch modes and any distinctive approaches to developer experience.

tasks.title: Competitor analysis: OpenCode (../opencode)
tasks.description: Independently analyze the OpenCode source at ../opencode. Review architecture, features, plugin/extension system, docs, CI, and UX. Produce: 1) target/temp/swarmout/opencode_report.md describing features, notable design decisions, strengths/weaknesses, and at least 6 concrete feature ideas for our product inspired by OpenCode; 2) target/temp/swarmout/opencode_summary.json enumerating feature availability, complexity, and priority. Note any novel integrations or automation patterns that would impact our roadmap.

tasks.title: Competitor analysis: roocode
tasks.description: Independently analyze Roocode (assume accessible repository or public project). Review its feature set, developer workflows, integrations, UI/CLI, docs, and license. Produce: 1) target/temp/swarmout/roocode_report.md listing features, UX insights, strengths/weaknesses, and at least 6 concrete features/behaviors to consider for our product; 2) target/temp/swarmout/roocode_summary.json with structured feature flags, estimated effort, and priority. Call out any unique selling points and any implementation shortcuts we might reuse or improve upon.

tasks.title: Create SWARMOUT2.md with feature additions, milestones and tasks
tasks.description: After s1–s5 are complete, aggregate their outputs and produce target/temp/swarmout/SWARMOUT2.md. This file must: 1) summarize the product review and each competitor analysis (one paragraph each), 2) list all recommended feature additions (one feature per subsection) with: description, why it's needed (rationale), which competitor inspired it (if any), priority (High/Medium/Low), rough effort estimate (in days or story points), and any technical or legal risks; 3) produce a concrete implementation plan organized into Milestones (3–6 milestones) with for each milestone: objective, included features, estimated duration, and success criteria; 4) break milestones into actionable tasks (developer tasks, docs, tests, QA, infra), each with estimated effort, dependencies (by task id within SWARMOUT2.md), and suggested owner role (e.g., backend engineer, CLI dev, UX); 5) provide a suggested 3-month timeline and resource estimate (headcount needed per sprint) and a short section on metrics to track success for each milestone; 6) include an appendix that lists source report filenames and short machine-readable fusion of the summary JSONs (merge product_report.json and all competitor summary.json files). Write the final SWARMOUT2.md to target/temp/swarmout/SWARMOUT2.md. Ensure the document is actionable and that all referenced files from s1–s5 are read and cited. Depends on: s1, s2, s3, s4, s5.
